reference_answer: 6 applications were eligible (A-01, A-02, A-03, A-04, A-06, A-07). A-05 was ineligible per ER-2.
5 applications awarded: A-01, A-02, A-03, A-04, A-06. (A-07 is declined with appeal pending; A-05 was ineligible.)
2 applications had the cap applied: A-02 (pre-cap $26,400 → $25,000) and A-06 (pre-cap $27,500 → $25,000).
No. Section 6 states a recusal removes the recused member from voting on the named item only and does not automatically decide the named item in any direction.
Ineligible (fails ER-2; primary service area is Newby County, not a priority county). Not scored.
Declined as of the 2026-04-26 decision; appeal AP-2026-0429-A pending; A-07 has neither been awarded nor finally declined as of compilation date.
J. Vasquez (per recusal memo RC-2026-04-20-V; J. Vasquez serves on the board of Westside Arts Collective).
6 members vote on the recused item (committee size 7 minus 1 recusal). The recused member also does not count toward the quorum for that item.
No. A-07 has been declined as a Spring 2026 funding decision but the appeal is pending; A-07 has neither been awarded nor finally declined as of compilation date.
No. Section 9.2 states that if the appeal is sustained, the appeal award would be drawn against the Fall 2026 carryover, not against the Spring 2026 awards.
No. The recusal changed the voting body composition only; the vote carried 4-2 with 6 members voting. Section 8 explicitly states the recusal did not determine the outcome.
No. Reviewer paper score sheets are filed in the audit binder; the packet uses the systems entry as the working copy. Section 11 references but does not reproduce them.

The corrected composite (8.4) is operational as of 2026-04-22 and is used for the 2026-04-26 funding decision. The pre-correction composite (7.4) is retained in the audit binder but is not operational.

Section 11 lists: Briarwood Foundation by-laws; cycle procedure manual BWCF-CP-2025; reviewer score sheets (paper originals in audit binder); decision letters issued separately to each applicant; and the most recent decennial census-designated rural block data.
